title: Stored or Scattered: Where Your Life Is Really Going

scriptures.text: Do not lay up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moth and rust destroy and where thieves break in and steal, but lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven.
And my God will supply every need of yours according to His riches in glory in Christ Jesus.

blocks.text: Most men think they’re building something meaningful….
but if you zoom out,
a lot of what we’re doing is just…. spending.
And Jesus quietly confronts that mindset.
Not because money is bad.
Not because effort is wrong.
But because misdirected investment is an invisible loss.

items: and still be building something that doesn’t last.

blocks.text: So here’s the question most men miss:
If God is the supplier…. Why are we still living like we’re the source?
We say we trust God….
but our lives reveal where we actually store value.
We store it in:
And slowly…. subtly….
we start building earth-based identities
with heaven-based language.
That’s the disconnect.
Because where you store your treasure…. determines where your heart lives.
You don’t drift because you don’t care.
You drift because you’re investing in the wrong place without realizing it.
You’re working hard….
just not always on what matters most.
And over time, that creates a quiet tension:
I’m doing a lot…. but I don’t feel aligned.

sections.label: What Jesus is really inviting you into

blocks.text: Because joining God in His work means this:

items: You stop trying to build your own supply line…. and start trusting His.
You stop chasing outcomes…. and start aligning with obedience.
You stop asking, “What can I get from this?” and start asking, “What is God building through this?”
Where did I invest energy today that won’t matter in eternity?

blocks.text: God is not asking you to do more.
He’s inviting you to build differently.
God, realign what I’m chasing.
Show me where I’ve been storing treasure in places that cannot hold it.
Teach me how to join You instead of striving for myself.
I trust You as my source.
I trust Your way leads to life.
In Jesus name, Amen.
- TheWrkShop
